project: DivFlow: Diverse LLM Response Selection via Sinkhorn-Guided Mechanism Design
triage_summary: Evaluation on 80 responses across 10 prompts is statistically underpowered; confidence intervals on coverage percentages would be wide

key_improvements.title: Scale evaluation dramatically
key_improvements.description: Increase benchmark from 30 responses/6 topics to 200 responses/15 topics across multiple seeds. Add multi-prompt evaluation (20 prompts). Compute power analysis to justify sample sizes. Report CIs on all metrics.
critiques_addressed: Small effective sample sizes yield wide CIs

key_improvements.title: Complete composition theorem proofs and add sensitivity analysis
key_improvements.description: Formalize the composition theorem with full proofs for (a) Lipschitz continuity of Sinkhorn divergence w.r.t. embedding perturbations, (b) epsilon-submodularity bound as function of regularization parameter, (c) tight epsilon-IC bound. Add sensitivity analysis for key hyperparameters (Sinkhorn epsilon, quality_weight lambda, Z3 grid resolution).

key_improvements.title: Fix Z3 verification gaps
key_improvements.description: Increase Z3 grid resolution from 5 to 15. Add Lipschitz-based soundness argument for continuous-to-discrete gap. Address 37.5% IC guarantee gap by characterizing uncertified agents. Add confidence intervals on VCG violation rates.
critiques_addressed: Grid resolution of 5 is extremely coarse
37.5% of agents lack formal IC guarantees
VCG violation rate needs confidence intervals

finalization: Update paper.tex with new results and proofs
